forecasts.reasoning:
The question asks if any member of the European Parliament will announce their resignation after June 9, 2025 and before July 1, 2025.
The knowledge cutoff is June 5, 2025, which gives a forecast horizon of just less than one month.
A major scandal is currently playing out. Belgian prosecutors have requested the lifting of the immunity of five MEPs due to their alleged involvement in a "cash-for-influence" bribery scandal linked to Huawei.
The request was announced around May 22, 2025. For one MEP (Giusi Princi), the request was reportedly withdrawn, leaving four under investigation (Attard, Minchev, Martusciello, De Meo). Wait, Daniel Attard is an S&D from Malta. 
The European Parliament will deliberate and vote on these remaining waivers around their June plenary session.
While the scandal and the investigations are severe enough to potentially lead to a resignation, it's relatively rare for MEPs to resign simply upon the prospect of immunity being lifted or early in a formal probe. Typically, they fight the probe, their immunity is lifted, and they might stand trial while retaining their seat, or they run out the clock. Resignations under pressure do happen, especially when a scandal significantly embarrasses their national party or the Parliament group.
Given the narrow window of exactly three weeks (June 9 - June 30) for the resolution criteria, the timing is tight. An MEP would have to decide to announce their resignation exactly during that window. Plenary takes place in June, and the vote on immunity might occur then, which could put maximal pressure on an MEP. However, resigning exactly within this relatively short 3-week window has maybe a 20-30% chance. I will guess 25%.

The phrase "reverse purchasing" (or "reverse purchase") appears to be a direct translation of a recent Chinese state media term (e.g., used by Global Times and People's Daily in April 2025) to describe a trend of foreign consumers buying Chinese goods. A site search against the New York Times returns zero historical use of either "reverse purchasing" or "reverse purchase," indicating that it is not standard terminology there (financially, NYT uses "reverse repurchase" or "reverse mortgage"). Given that NYT rarely adopts niche literal translations of Chinese media jargon without specific reason, and more importantly because the resolution period is an extremely narrow 21-day window (June 10 to June 30, 2025), the probability of the NYT using this exact phrase in that specific timeframe is extremely low.

X/Twitter is currently blocked in China, Iran, Myanmar, North Korea, Russia, Turkmenistan, and Venezuela. The main source of uncertainty is Venezuela, which instituted a '10-day ban' in August 2024 following disputed elections. This ban remains active as of early 2025. Given Elon Musk's public dispute with Nicolás Maduro and Musk's prominent role in U.S. politics, it is highly unlikely Maduro will capitulate or lift the ban before July 2025. There is no sign that any of the deeply entrenched regimes (China, Iran, North Korea, Russia, Myanmar, Turkmenistan) will unblock the platform. The other, minor risk is Wikipedia refactoring the 'Censorship of Twitter' page so that one or more countries fail to be explicitly listed as 'blocked,' but Metaculus admins typically look for the spirit of the text and won't penalize minor formatting or section-header changes. Therefore, my probability that all 7 countries will still be listed as 'currently blocked' is 75%.

The question is whether the government of Uruguay will announce that its president, Yamandú Orsi, will attend the BRICS summit in July 2025 before the resolution deadline of July 1, 2025. 

During Orsi's inauguration in early March 2025, Brazilian President Lula da Silva formally invited Uruguay, Mexico, and Colombia to attend the upcoming BRICS summit in Rio de Janeiro in July 2025. Multiple reports indicate that Orsi showed active interest in participating to promote multilateralism and free trade. Most significantly, an article from *El País Uruguay* published on March 3, 2025, explicitly states: "El presidente Yamandú Orsi se dispone a viajar a mediados de julio a Río de Janeiro" (President Yamandú Orsi is preparing to travel in mid-July to Rio de Janeiro) in the context of his presence at the BRICS summit. 

Given that *El País* is a major Uruguayan newspaper reporting on the president's schedule just days after his inauguration and citing his preparation to travel for this exact event, it operates strongly as public communication of plans to attend. The resolution criteria is met by "any sort of public communication (written or oral) confirming its acceptance of the invitation for President Yamandú Orsi to attend in person." Since a specific, reputable Uruguayan news source has already reported that Orsi is preparing to travel to Rio de Janeiro in July, and the cutoff is June 5, 2025, it confirms that before July 1, 2025, the intent to attend has been publicly reported based on government communications. 

I'll put the probability high but not entirely certain, as the El País wording "se dispone a viajar" might still need a completely official press release from the Presidential website or Ministry of Foreign Affairs, but usually these leaks to major papers count as an announcement on behalf of the government, or the official announcement is imminent. I estimate an 83% chance that either this report itself counts or a more formal press release has happened/will happen closely before the July 1, 2025 deadline.

Francis Suarez has publicly floated the idea of running for Florida governor since mid-2024 and restated his interest in May 2025 during an Axios/The Race event and on Fox & Friends. At the event in May, he specifically mentioned he hasn't made a decision but is considering it. However, he specifically acknowledged that Trump's strong backing of Representative Byron Donalds makes the path difficult for other Republicans, an implicit admission that he may lack viability and potentially look for "other ways to serve," like an administration appointment. Additionally, the Republican primary field features prominent contenders like Donalds and potentially Casey DeSantis and Matt Gaetz. His short-lived 2024 presidential campaign and Trump's endorsement of someone else show his relative weakness. 

The resolution date is exactly July 1, 2025, which gives roughly 25 days since the forecast cutoff (June 5). While candidates do sometimes announce in May/June of the off-year, doing so requires organizing infrastructure under a relatively compact timeline to announce in just over three weeks. Given his recent hesitancy regarding Trump's endorsement of Donalds and no clear report of setting up exploratory structures or hiring specific gubernatorial campaign managers right now, it is fairly unlikely he pulls the trigger and formally announces his candidacy before July 1. I'll estimate 25% – there is a chance he wants to immediately launch to elevate his profile or secure leverage, but fundamentally the Trump dynamic strongly pushes him out.

As of the June 5, 2025 knowledge cutoff, Casey DeSantis has not announced a candidacy for Florida's 2026 gubernatorial election. Rather than gearing up for an imminent campaign launch, recent reporting (May 2025) strongly indicates she is either leaning against running or delaying any decision until the fall. Following Donald Trump's endorsement of U.S. Representative Byron Donalds, who has formally entered the race and opened up a 19-point polling lead, political momentum for a Casey DeSantis bid has significantly cooled. On May 20, she explicitly stated she is in "no hurry" to launch a run, noting that she has "plenty of time down the road to be able to make that decision" because the qualifying deadline is still a year away. Furthermore, an investigation into her "Hope Florida" charity in late spring further complicated any immediate campaign rollout. Given that there are only about 3.5 weeks remaining between the cutoff date and the July 1, 2025 deadline, and considering her public commitment to taking her time as well as operative consensus that any decision will be delayed until autumn (if she runs at all), the likelihood of a formal announcement before July 1 is very low.
